verses.text: All the people gather as one and ask Ezra to bring the book of the law, and he reads it from morning till midday, the people attentive. They hunger for God's word. A true revival begins when a people long to hear and heed the Scriptures.

All the people gather as one and ask Ezra to bring the book of the law, and he reads it from morning till midday, the people attentive. They hunger for God's word. A true revival begins when a people long to hear and heed the Scriptures.

All the people gather as one and ask Ezra to bring the book of the law, and he reads it from morning till midday, the people attentive. They hunger for God's word. A true revival begins when a people long to hear and heed the Scriptures.

Ezra stands on a wooden pulpit, opens the book in the sight of all, blesses the LORD, and the people lift their hands, answer 'Amen, Amen,' and bow with their faces to the ground. Reverence surrounds the reading. God's word is to be received in awe and worship, not idle curiosity.

Ezra stands on a wooden pulpit, opens the book in the sight of all, blesses the LORD, and the people lift their hands, answer 'Amen, Amen,' and bow with their faces to the ground. Reverence surrounds the reading. God's word is to be received in awe and worship, not idle curiosity.

Ezra stands on a wooden pulpit, opens the book in the sight of all, blesses the LORD, and the people lift their hands, answer 'Amen, Amen,' and bow with their faces to the ground. Reverence surrounds the reading. God's word is to be received in awe and worship, not idle curiosity.

The Levites read distinctly and give the sense, helping the people understand the reading. Scripture is opened, not merely recited. God's word does its work when it is explained so that hearers grasp its meaning.

The Levites read distinctly and give the sense, helping the people understand the reading. Scripture is opened, not merely recited. God's word does its work when it is explained so that hearers grasp its meaning.

As the people weep under the word, their leaders say, 'this day is holy... mourn not... the joy of the LORD is your strength,' and send them to feast and share with the poor. Conviction gives way to holy joy. Godly sorrow is meant to lead, not to lasting gloom, but to gladness in the Lord.

As the people weep under the word, their leaders say, 'this day is holy... mourn not... the joy of the LORD is your strength,' and send them to feast and share with the poor. Conviction gives way to holy joy. Godly sorrow is meant to lead, not to lasting gloom, but to gladness in the Lord.

Next day the heads study the law, find the command for the feast of tabernacles, and proclaim it, gathering branches. Fresh obedience springs from fresh study. Searching the Scriptures shows God's people duties long forgotten and stirs them to keep them.

Next day the heads study the law, find the command for the feast of tabernacles, and proclaim it, gathering branches. Fresh obedience springs from fresh study. Searching the Scriptures shows God's people duties long forgotten and stirs them to keep them.

Next day the heads study the law, find the command for the feast of tabernacles, and proclaim it, gathering branches. Fresh obedience springs from fresh study. Searching the Scriptures shows God's people duties long forgotten and stirs them to keep them.

They build booths and keep the feast with a gladness unseen since Joshua's day, while Ezra reads the law daily. Joy and the word go together throughout the feast. When God's people obey His word wholeheartedly, their worship overflows with joy.
